name: Typewriter Effect
summary: A text component that animates text appearing letter-by-letter with a blinking cursor, simulating typing.
detailedDescription: Typewriter Effect creates a realistic typing animation where text appears character by character with staggered timing. The component splits each word into individual characters and wraps them in motion spans that animate from hidden/transparent to visible inline-block elements. Using Framer Motion's useAnimate hook and stagger timing, each letter appears with a 0.1 second delay, creating a natural typing rhythm. A blinking cursor (blue rectangular bar) animates continuously with opacity pulsing to simulate a real text cursor. The animation only triggers when the component comes into view using useInView detection. Text scales responsively from base size on mobile to 5xl on large screens with bold typography. The component accepts an array of word objects, each with text and optional className for per-word styling customization. This effect is ideal for hero headings, code-related content, terminal-style interfaces, or any context where a typing metaphor enhances the message.
purpose: Simulate realistic typing animation for text content
Create engaging text reveals that capture attention
Add terminal or code-editor aesthetic to interfaces
behavior: Animates letters individually with staggered 0.1s delays
Triggers animation only when component enters viewport
Displays continuous blinking cursor at text end
Splits words into character arrays for granular animation
Transitions letters from hidden to inline-block with opacity fade
visualCharacteristics: Blue blinking cursor bar at text end
Dark text on light or light text on dark
Smooth opacity transitions for each character
recommendations: Use where moderate motion and reusable composition improve UX and clarity.
